name: Plasencia, Gargüera, and Cabrero Loop
name_original: Plasencia desde Casas del Castañar — circular por el Cerro Bullón — la Sierra de San Bernabé
description: This moderate 32.6-mile (52.4 km) road cycling route from Casas del Castañar offers panoramic views of the Jerte Valley and Plasencia.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Casas Del Castañar are situated in the Valle del Jerte, within the foothills of the Sierra de Gredos and on the southern slope of the Sierra de San Bernabé. This location provides a diverse landscape characterized by mountainous terrain and valleys. Cyclists can expect routes winding through areas with chestnut and cherry trees, offering varied gradients. The region's elevation at 675 meters contributes to routes with both climbs and descents. Best road cycling routes around Casas Del Castañar The most popular road cycling route is Plasencia, Gargüera, and Cabrero Loop, a 32.6 miles (52.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 46 minutes to complete. This route features significant elevation gain through mountainous terrain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Piornal Pass from Valdastillas, a moderate 21.0 miles (33.8 km) path. This route offers ascents through the region's varied landscape. Local road cyclists also love the Piornal Climbs Loop, a 21.5 miles (34.5 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 0 minutes. Road cycling around Casas Del Castañar is defined by mountainous terrain, valleys, and areas with chestnut and cherry trees. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Casas Del Castañar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 8 reviews. More than 1000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Casas Del Castañar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Casas Del Castañar?
A: There are over 30 road cycling routes around Casas Del Castañar, offering a variety of options for different skill levels. The region features 15 moderate routes and 17 difficult routes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling around Casas Del Castañar?
A: Road cycling routes in Casas Del Castañar are characterized by mountainous terrain and valleys, set within the foothills of the Sierra de Gredos and on the southern slope of the Sierra de San Bernabé. You'll cycle through areas rich with chestnut and cherry trees, enjoying varied gradients and magnificent views over the Jerte River and its valley.

Q: Are there challenging climbs for experienced road cyclists in the region?
A: Yes, the region offers several challenging climbs. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20141155" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Honduras Pass – Puerto de Béjar loop from Asador Hotel Balneario Valle del Jerte&lt;/a&gt; feature significant elevation gains, with over 3300 meters of ascent, making them suitable for advanced riders seeking demanding routes.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in Casas Del Castañar?
A: The road cycling routes around Casas Del Castañar are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 8 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the scenic beauty of the Valle del Jerte, and the challenging climbs available.

Q: Can I see any natural attractions like waterfalls while cycling?
A: Yes, several natural attractions are accessible near the cycling routes. You can visit the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4204503" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nogaleas Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4360769" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caozo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, both of which are popular natural monuments in the area.

Q: Are there any notable viewpoints along the road cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its stunning vistas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5638081" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Panoramic view of the Jerte Valley&lt;/a&gt; offers breathtaking scenery, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2684422" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Piornal Pass&lt;/a&gt; is another excellent spot for expansive views, often included in popular cycling loops.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling options in Casas Del Castañar?
A: While the routes around Casas Del Castañar are predominantly moderate to difficult with significant elevation changes, there are no specifically designated 'easy' road cycling routes listed. However, families with older, experienced children might find some of the moderate routes manageable, especially if focusing on shorter sections or less steep areas. Always check the route profile for elevation and distance before setting out.

Q: What is a popular moderate road cycling route in the area?
A: A popular moderate route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1292300456/piornal-pass-from-valdastillas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Piornal Pass from Valdastillas&lt;/a&gt;. This 33.8 km (21.0 miles) path offers ascents through the region's varied landscape and is a favorite among local road cyclists.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1292442316/plasencia-gargueera-and-cabrero-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plasencia, Gargüera, and Cabrero Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a 52.4 km (32.6 miles) trail that takes you through mountainous terrain and returns to your starting point. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1292360492/piornal-climbs-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Piornal Climbs Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 34.5 km (21.5 miles) long.

Q: What other natural features can I explore near the cycling routes?
A: Beyond waterfalls and viewpoints, you can also explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5391554" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Plasencia&lt;/a&gt; (Plasencia Reservoir) and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5638455" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural pools of Valdastillas&lt;/a&gt;. These offer additional natural beauty and opportunities for relaxation or further exploration off the bike.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Casas Del Castañar?
A: The region's abundance of chestnut and cherry trees creates vibrant scenery throughout the seasons. Spring, particularly during the cherry blossom season, offers stunning views. Autumn, with its rich fall colors, is also highly recommended. Summers can be warm, while winters might present cooler temperatures in the mountainous areas.

Q: Is there a particularly long or challenging route for advanced cyclists?
A: For advanced cyclists seeking a significant challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20136960" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of Memory – Puerto de Béjar loop from Regino&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding option. This route spans over 137 km (85 miles) and includes nearly 3000 meters of elevation gain, offering a full day of intense riding.
tips: Spectacular views of the Plasencia Reservoir and the valley of the Jerte River below. The path passes next to the river dam.
A small town at the foot of the Sierra de San Bernabé, the town of Gargüera de La Vera is surrounded by cereal fields and oak forests.
The road turns upwards after passing the small town of Cabrero and the views of the Jerte Valley begin to open up with the Sierra de la Cabrera in the distance.

faqs.question: How challenging is this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as moderate and requires good fitness due to its significant elevation gain of approximately 888 meters. You can expect sustained climbs, particularly as it traverses the Sierra de San Bernabé and includes Cerro Bullón, which reaches 1117 meters.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The route is generally characterized by mostly well-paved surfaces, making it suitable for road cyclists. However, be prepared for mountainous terrain with significant ascents and descents as you navigate the Sierra de San Bernabé.

faqs.question: What are the key highlights and viewpoints along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter beautiful natural landscapes and charming villages. Notable highlights include the Plasencia Reservoir and the settlement of Gargüera de la Vera. Keep an eye out for the View of the Jerte Valley from the Cherry Fields, and panoramic vistas of the lower Jerte Valley, the Riscos de Villavieja, and Plasencia from higher elevations like Era de San Bernabé.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Given the mountainous terrain and potential for heat, spring and autumn are generally the best seasons for cycling this route. The weather is typically milder, and the natural scenery, especially the cherry blossoms in spring or autumn foliage, can be particularly stunning.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as moderate and requires good fitness due to its significant elevation gain and sustained climbs. It is generally not recommended for beginners or young families, who might find the ascents too challenging.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this cycling route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details aren't provided, Casas del Castañar, being the starting point, would be the most logical place to look for parking. Look for public parking areas within the village or nearby.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with any other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with several other paths. You'll find sections that overlap with the [CIMA CC02] Piornal * Cabrero, Paseo Fluvial Plasencia, Aguas Arriba, and La era de San Bernabé, among others.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route, so while there aren't specific restrictions on dogs, it's generally not suitable for cycling with pets unless they are in a carrier designed for bikes. Always ensure your dog's safety and comfort.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no special permits or entrance fees required to cycle this public road cycling route through the Sierra de San Bernabé.

faqs.question: Are there places to refuel or rest along the route?
faqs.answer: The route passes through settlements like Gargüera de la Vera and starts from Casas del Castañar. These villages are likely to offer opportunities for refreshments, cafes, or rest stops. It's always a good idea to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially for the more remote sections.

faqs.question: What equipment is recommended for this road cycling tour?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and elevation gain, a well-maintained road bike is essential. We recommend carrying plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit, a helmet, and appropriate cycling attire. Due to the mountainous sections, consider gearing suitable for climbs.

waypoints.name: View of the Jerte Valley from the Cherry Fields
